task_completion.description: Log into ADAudit Plus and access the dashboard or reports.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window showing the ADAudit Plus interface, indicating successful login.

task_completion.description: Investigate the audit trail to find the targeted local user account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ADAudit Plus reports or search interface showing failed logon events, specifically highlighting the user 'rwilliams' with multiple failed attempts (around 15).

task_completion.description: Create a new technician account for the incident response team.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ADAudit Plus Admin or Technician configuration screen showing a new user 'incident_handler' with the name 'Incident Handler', email 'handler@corp.local', and role 'Operator'.

task_completion.description: Configure event collection alerts to the security team.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ADAudit Plus alert or notification settings screen showing the email 'security-alerts@corp.local' configured with a frequency of 1 hour.

task_completion.description: Document the findings in the specified text file on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad) open with the file 'brute_force_analysis.txt' containing the account name 'rwilliams', the approximate number of failed logons (15), and a recommended response action.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web GUI to interact with ADAudit Plus.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the ADAudit Plus web interface in a browser, rather than using terminal commands or API scripts to configure settings.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually viewed the failed logon reports before writing the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be at least one screenshot showing the ADAudit Plus report/audit trail with 'rwilliams' and the failed logons visible, proving the agent didn't just guess or hallucinate the privileged information.

integrity.description: Verify the analysis report was created properly in the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file 'brute_force_analysis.txt' should be visible on the Desktop or open in a standard GUI text editor like Notepad, rather than just being echoed to a file via command prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The primary target account is 'rwilliams' with exactly 15 failed logons. The new technician account must have the username 'incident_handler', role 'Operator', and email 'handler@corp.local'. Event collection alerts must be configured to send to 'security-alerts@corp.local' with a 1-hour frequency. The final report must be saved at 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\brute_force_analysis.txt' and include 'rwilliams', the number 15 (or approximate), and a recommended response action.
